drill: ED-n — edit/delete real-backend smoke: /edit supersession + re-embed (D-116), reply-targeted /delete + NOTE->DRAFT tombstone (D-117), operator-only audited hard-delete (D-117); proves the §6 exit criterion of docs/EDIT-DELETE-ROADMAP.md (ED-n-prep template / ED-n capture)

metadata.environment: real backend — STORAGE_BACKEND=postgres (migrations through 0010 applied), EMBEDDING_BACKEND=openai, EMBEDDING_MODEL=text-embedding-3-large, EMBEDDING_DIMENSION=3072, CHAT_BACKEND=openai, CHAT_MODEL=gpt-4.1; OP-2 bounded retry/backoff active at defaults; webhook secret set; MEMORY_RAG_PG_TEST_DSN set for the ED-2/ED-3 Postgres acceptance legs
metadata.notes: Live operator evidence for the edit/delete milestone close. Per the redaction rule (RUNBOOK §"Edit/delete real-backend drill (ED-n)"), credential-bearing values (OPENAI_API_KEY, TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN, TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET, PUBLIC_HOSTNAME if used, webhook URL token) are replaced by <REDACTED> placeholders or a _redacted: true flag; structural outcomes (status strings, log-line shapes, row counts, lifecycle_state values, supersedes_* lineage, audit counts) are preserved verbatim. Operator action required before commit: (1) copy this file to docs/edit-delete-drill/edit-delete-smoke-<YYYYMMDD>-evidence.json (UTC-dated), (2) drop the top-level "_template": true flag, (3) replace every <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR> placeholder with the verbatim captured observation, (4) run the redaction grep checklist from the RUNBOOK subsection.

preflight_state.boot_log_line_shape: app.created env=<env> version=<version> embedding_backend=openai embedding_dim=3072 chat_backend=openai chat_model=gpt-4.1
preflight_state.boot_log_line_verbatim: <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR — verbatim app.created line from docker compose logs app; expected: embedding_backend=openai embedding_dim=3072 chat_backend=openai chat_model=gpt-4.1>
preflight_state.migrations_head: <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR — expected: 0010.note-chunk-lifecycle-state applied (the lifecycle_state + supersedes_* columns exist)>
preflight_state.boot_gate_notes: _verify_embedding_contour and _verify_chat_contour in src/memory_rag/app.py abort start on any canonical-knob mismatch (R-10); a successful app.created line implies both gates passed.

edit_round_trip.purpose: ED-2 (D-116): an /edit of a captured /note supersedes the prior active revision (lineage recorded, prior flipped to superseded), the new revision re-embeds, and the superseded revision is excluded from retrieval immediately (R-4).

seed_note_outcome.prior_note_id: <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR — notes.note_id of the seeded active revision>
seed_note_outcome.prior_chunk_id: <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR — event_chunks.chunk_id of the seeded active revision>

message.message_id: <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR — SAME message_id as the seed note>
message.edit_date: <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR — unix seconds, LATER than date; becomes edit_seq, the new idempotency key>
message.text: <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR — e.g. "/note 2026-05-09\nWalked the dog along the river" (edited NOTE content)>

edit_round_trip.supersession_log_line_verbatim: <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR — verbatim edit.superseded line from app logs>

post_edit_state.new_note_id: <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR — notes.note_id of the new active revision>
post_edit_state.i6_authorship_preserved: <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR — expected: true; both revisions carry the same author_user_id>

retrieval_check_after_edit.ask_request_text: <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR — e.g. "/ask river" — a query matching the EDITED content>
retrieval_check_after_edit.edited_text_retrievable: <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR — expected: true; the new active revision is returned>
retrieval_check_after_edit.superseded_text_excluded: <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR — expected: true; the prior revision is not returned on either leg (R-4)>

edit_round_trip.provider_attempt_log_line_verbatim: <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR — verbatim provider.attempt line for the re-embed; attempt=2/3 outcome=success is acceptable if a transient 429 occurred>

delete_round_trip.purpose: ED-3 (D-117): a reply-targeted /delete tombstones the active note + chunk (retained with I-6 authorship, excluded from retrieval immediately — R-4); a NOTE->DRAFT edit tombstones the prior active note; every miss is a friendly fail-closed no-op; idempotent.

reply_to_message.message_id: <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR — message_id of a saved /note to delete>

delete_outcome.reply_text_verbatim: <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR — expected: "Deleted. That note is removed from search and won't appear in answers.">
delete_outcome.i6_authorship_preserved: <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR — expected: true; the tombstoned rows are retained with author_user_id intact>

delete_round_trip.delete_log_line_verbatim: <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR — verbatim delete.tombstoned line from app logs>

retrieval_check_after_delete.ask_request_text: <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR — a query that matched the deleted note's content>
retrieval_check_after_delete.tombstoned_text_excluded: <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR — expected: true; the tombstoned chunk is not returned on either leg (R-4)>

fail_closed_no_op_checks.delete_with_no_reply_target_reply_verbat: <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR — expected: "To delete a note, reply to it with /delete.">
fail_closed_no_op_checks.delete_replying_to_non_note_reply_verbat: <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR — expected: "Nothing to delete — reply to a saved note with /delete to remove it.">
fail_closed_no_op_checks.idempotent_second_delete: <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR — expected: a second /delete of the same note is a no-op ("Nothing to delete …"); already-tombstoned row unchanged>

note_to_draft_removal_sub_leg.purpose: Editing a captured /note to drop its /note command re-routes the edited delivery to DRAFT and tombstones the prior active note (ED-3 / D-117).

message.message_id: <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR — SAME message_id as a still-active seeded /note>
message.text: <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR — plain text WITHOUT the /note command, e.g. "never mind">

note_to_draft_removal_sub_leg.delete_log_line_verbatim: <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR — verbatim delete.tombstoned line for this NOTE->DRAFT removal>

hard_delete.purpose: ED-3 (D-117): operator-only audited hard-delete physically removes the raw source aggregate and its derived rows in FK-safe order, community-scoped, with a structured audit.hard_delete log record. There is NO operator-facing CLI today — the method is reachable only as a DomainService call; a hard-delete CLI is a deferred follow-up (reconciliation-CLI pattern).
hard_delete.invocation: inline python snippet (no CLI) — builds the Postgres-backed DomainService exactly as adapters/telegram/webhook.py does (PostgresDomainStore(settings.postgres_dsn()) + build_embedding_client(settings) + DomainService(store, embedding_client=...)), then calls svc.hard_delete_source_message(<source_message_id>, community_id=<cid>, requested_by="operator"); see the RUNBOOK procedure for the exact snippet
hard_delete.audit_log_line_shape: audit.hard_delete community_id=<cid> requested_by=<who> source_message_id=<id> source_messages=<n> notes=<n> event_chunks=<n> embedding_records=<n> retrieval_hits=<n>
hard_delete.audit_log_line_verbatim: <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR — verbatim audit.hard_delete line from app/script logs>
hard_delete.fk_safe_order_observed: <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR — expected: retrieval_hits -> embedding_records -> event_chunks -> notes -> source_messages, no FK violation>
hard_delete.post_delete_rows_absent: <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR — expected: true; the source_message_id and all its derived rows are physically gone>

pg_acceptance_legs.purpose: The ED-2/ED-3 Postgres acceptance legs written in ED-2/ED-3 but not yet run (MEMORY_RAG_PG_TEST_DSN unset in the dev environment) are executed as part of this drill.
pg_acceptance_legs.command: MEMORY_RAG_PG_TEST_DSN=<REDACTED> uv run pytest tests/test_edit_supersession_ingest.py tests/test_delete_tombstone.py
pg_acceptance_legs.test_edit_supersession_ingest_postgres_l: <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR — expected: passed (the postgres-parametrized fixtures run, incl. test_superseded_prior_excluded_from_retrieval_after_edit)>

out_of_scope_for_this_packet: Any src/ / scripts / docker-compose.yml / schema / migration / tests/ change.
A hard-delete operator CLI / control surface (deferred follow-up; this drill invokes the method via an inline python snippet).
The /start and /help wording change to mention /delete (deferred).
Any live OpenAI call inside make check or any new gated test.
Any harness extension, new aggregate, or retrieval/answer-path behavior change.
Resilience-knob tuning beyond D-047 / D-049 defaults.
Multi-run / multi-query / multi-language statistical capture — the milestone is a bounded value-loop proof of the edit/delete contract.

summary.edit_round_trip_green: <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR — expected: true if the /edit superseded the prior revision (prior=superseded, lineage recorded), the new revision is active + re-embedded (embedding_status=ready), and the superseded text is excluded from retrieval (R-4)>
summary.delete_round_trip_green: <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR — expected: true if reply-targeted /delete and the NOTE->DRAFT edit both tombstoned the active note+chunk (retained, I-6 intact), the tombstoned text is excluded from retrieval (R-4), and the fail-closed no-op / idempotency replies match>
summary.hard_delete_audited: <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR — expected: true if hard_delete_source_message physically removed the aggregate in FK-safe order and emitted the audit.hard_delete record with non-zero counts>
summary.superseded_and_tombstoned_excluded_from_: <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR — expected: true; the generalized active-state filter (R-4) excludes both non-active states on both legs>
summary.pg_acceptance_legs_green: <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR — expected: true if the ED-2/ED-3 postgres-gated test legs passed under MEMORY_RAG_PG_TEST_DSN>
summary.closes_edit_delete_milestone: <TO_FILL_BY_OPERATOR — true only when every leg above is green>
